title: Morgan Stanley carves out European AI as its own trade
subtitle: Morgan Stanley chief European equity strategist Marina Zavolock said she has strong conviction on Europe's AI trade, citing outstanding fundamentals still rising. The call positions European AI as a standalone investment theme, not just a US-centric story.

title: Meta picks Reliance for first India AI data center
subtitle: Meta signed its first India AI data center deal with Reliance, securing a 168-megawatt facility to support its global AI computing needs. The deal marks Meta's entry into India's data center market and can be expanded over time.

title: Paramount’s Middle East Billions in Warner Bid Get EU Probe
subtitle: Paramount Skydance's $110 billion takeover bid for Warner Bros. Discovery is under EU review under the Foreign Subsidies Regulation. Regulators are probing Middle Eastern funds helping to bankroll the acquisition, signaling tighter scrutiny of foreign-backed media deals.

title: Shopee cuts hundreds of developers in AI pivot
subtitle: Sea Ltd.'s Shopee is cutting hundreds of developer jobs globally as it adopts AI and builds new AI services, joining rivals worldwide in reducing tech headcount. The cuts reflect a broader industry pattern of AI adoption reshaping workforce needs.

title: ASML's record price hides a decade-low valuation gap
subtitle: ASML hit fresh record highs, yet its forward P/E ratio versus major peers sits at the lowest level in more than a decade. The gap between price momentum and relative valuation points to investor skepticism about its growth premium.

title: Rotomate raises €2.1M to replace industrial analysts
subtitle: Finnish AI startup Rotomate raised €2.1M in pre-seed funding led by Kvanted, with participation from Robin Capital, Angel Invest, and Accel. The startup builds tools to automate industrial reliability analysis and predict equipment failures in manufacturing.

title: Carlyle sees M&A deal wave building into year-end
subtitle: Carlyle Co-Head of American Private Equity Steve Wise says deal activity is picking up, with credit markets in good shape and a favorable backdrop for M&A. His outlook signals confidence in US economic strength through year-end.

title: Google's $35 billion backstop turns Anthropic lease into a loan
subtitle: Anthropic is leasing computer chips across five data centers, with Google backstopping the lease payments to enable what amounts to a $35 billion loan. The arrangement deepens Google's financial ties to one of its earliest AI portfolio companies.

title: Chrome pulls the flag that let uBlock Origin survive
subtitle: Google Chrome is removing all uBlock Origin bypasses via the upcoming Manifest V3 changes, with Microsoft Edge and Opera expected to follow. This effectively ends ad-blocking workarounds that circumvent Chrome's Web Request API restrictions.

title: TSMC sales up 30% on AI chip orders
subtitle: TSMC reported a 30% rise in monthly sales, driven by sustained demand for AI chips. The figure underscores how the global push to build AI infrastructure continues to fuel semiconductor spending.

title: D-Wave pushes into gate-model quantum with dual-rail encoding
subtitle: D-Wave is pursuing gate-model quantum computing using dual-rail qubit encoding, expanding beyond its quantum annealing heritage. The approach positions the company to compete more directly with IBM, Google, and other gate-model rivals.

title: Einride's Nasdaq debut rides SpaceX's IPO wave
subtitle: Einride AB, a Swedish driverless truck company, begins trading on Nasdaq in New York on Wednesday. The listing is timed to capture investor enthusiasm ahead of SpaceX's IPO later this week.
